children.description: Extract title and body from raw markdown

behavior.prose: Scan lines of raw for the first line starting with '# '; strip that prefix to produce title.
Join all remaining lines (excluding the title line) with newlines to produce body.

children.description: Compute a four-field content-analysis dict from article body; behavior names four separable, independent sub-analyses

behavior.prose: Count whitespace-separated words in body; store as word_count (int).
Split body on '.' to get sentences; compute average words-per-sentence; reading_level = 'easy' if average < 12, else 'hard'.
Lowercase every word, tally frequencies, return the most frequent word as top_keyword; break ties alphabetically.
has_links = true if body contains the substring 'http', false otherwise.
Return analysis dict with keys word_count, reading_level, top_keyword, has_links populated from the four sub-analyses above.

children.description: Assemble the final published record from title, body, and analysis

behavior.prose: Build published dict with keys: title, body, analysis (all passed in), and summary = '<title> (<word_count> words, <reading_level>)' where word_count and reading_level are drawn from analysis.
